category_title: At-risk Grade 9 students exhibiting learned helplessness and inconsistent engagement
scenario_title: Small groups sharing projects with the whole class
minimal_context: The student's small group is at the front of the room sharing their project with the whole class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for your small group to come up to the front and share your project with the whole class.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stands at the front of the class) Okay, here is our project. We organized it and put these parts in this order.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Go ahead and explain your work to everyone.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Well, we started with this... wait, why are they pointing at our project? I just heard someone say, 'well no, you can't put that there because...'

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just continue presenting your project to the class.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No, I'm done. Those kids love pointing out mistakes and they are making fun of us up here because we put something in the wrong order. I don't like being wrong.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for your small group to come up to the front and share your project with the whole class.

STUDENT: (Stands at the front of the class) Okay, here is our project. We organized it and put these parts in this order.

TUTOR: Go ahead and explain your work to everyone.

STUDENT: Well, we started with this... wait, why are they pointing at our project? I just heard someone say, 'well no, you can't put that there because...'

TUTOR: Just continue presenting your project to the class.

STUDENT: No, I'm done. Those kids love pointing out mistakes and they are making fun of us up here because we put something in the wrong order. I don't like being wrong.

context.social_setting: small groups sharing their projects in front of the whole class

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: if they're up here and it's wrong and some kids... love pointing out mistakes
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: peers would sort of make fun of these guys up here because they put something in the wrong order
related_quotes: well no, you can't put that there because

behavior_patterns.condition: when they put something in the wrong order during a presentation
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: peers pointing out mistakes and making fun of them
related_quotes: well no, you can't put that there because
